title: Sorbus amelanchier

side_effects: Sorbus amelanchier, also known as the Saskatoon serviceberry, is generally considered safe for consumption, but excessive intake may cause gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea. In rare cases, Sorbus amelanchier berries have been known to cause allergic reactions in sensitive individuals, leading to symptoms like hives, itching, or swelling. The seeds of the plant contain amygdalin, a compound that can release cyanide when ingested, potentially causing cyanide poisoning if consumed in large quantities. However, the risk of cyanide poisoning is relatively low, as the seeds must be ingested in significant amounts to be toxic. It's essential to consume Sorbus amelanchier berries in moderation and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
